name: Strawberry Rhubarb Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Rhubarb can be fresh or frozen; use fresh strawberries.
2. Stand for 10 minutes.
3. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
4. Combine rhubarb and grape juice in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat to low.
5. Simmer, uncovered, until rhubarb is tender (8-10 minutes for fresh or 5 minutes for frozen); drain.
6. In a medium bowl, combine cornstarch, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt; mix well.
7. Add strawberries and toss to coat. Stir in cooked rhubarb and fruit spread.
8. Roll out one pie crust to an 11-inch circle and line a 9-inch pie plate with it. Trim the pastry and flute edges, sealing to the edge of the pie plate.
9. Fill the shell with the fruit mixture and dot with fruit spread.
10. Roll out remaining pastry to a 10-inch circle. Cut into 1/2-inch strips.
11. Form into a lattice design over the fruit.
12. In a small bowl, combine egg yolks and sour cream until well-blended. Brush over the pastry.
13. Bake at 450°F (230°C) for 10 minutes.
14. Reduce oven to 350°F (175°C). Continue baking for 30 minutes, until the pastry is golden brown and filling is hot and bubbly. Cover loosely with foil during the last 30 minutes of baking if desired.
15. Cool on a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.
